title: Safety system for a vehicle comprising an electronic identification element
abstract: A Safety system for a vehicle is described. Said safety system has a restraining device with a restraining element and an inflator (20) inflating or tensioning this restraining element, and an electronic identification element (so called dongle) being adapted for being identified by a controller (48) of the vehicle. In order to prevent that am electronic identification element of an already fired restraining device is re-used in a copied or stolen restraining device, the electronic identification element (42a, 42b) is destroyed when the inflator (20) is triggered.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. Safety system for a vehicle, said safety system having a restraining device with a restraining element and an inflator (20, 70) inflating or tensioning this restraining element, and an electronic identification element (40) being adapted for being identified by a controller of the vehicle, characterized in that the electronic identification element (40) is destroyed when the inflator (20) is triggered. 2, Safety system according to claim 1 , characterized in that the electronic identification element (40) is destroyed due to a movement and/or a deformation of the restraining device or a part of it. 3. Safety system according to claim 2, characterized in that a first section of the electronic identification element (40) is mechanically coupled to a section of a first element of the restraining device and a second section of the electronic identification element (40) is mechanically coupled to a second element of the restraining device or to a section of the first element remote from the section of the first element to which the first section of the elec- tronic identification element (40) is coupled. 4. Safety system according to claim 1 , characterized in that the electronic identification system is destroyed due to a gas stream generated by the inflator (20) and hitting the electronic identification element (40). 5. Safety system according to claim 1 or to claim 4, characterized in that the inflator (20, 70) is a pyrotechnic gas generator and that the electronic identification element (40) is destroyed due to the heat generated by the gas generator. 6. Safety system according to claim 1 , characterized in that the inflator (20, 70) is electrically connected to at least two electrical conductors (26a, 26b) and that the electronic identification element (40) is electrically connected 

 to those conductors (26a, 26b) such that when an ignition voltage is applied to the conductors (26a, 26b), the electronic identification element (40) is electrically destroyed due to this ignition voltage. 7, Safety system according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the safety system is an airbag module with the restraining element being an airbag. 8. Safety assembly according to claim 3 and to claim 7, characterized in that the first element is the airbag (30). 9. Safety system according to claim 3 and to claim 7, characterized in that the first element is a door (14) that opens when the airbag (30) deploys. 10. Safety system according to one of the claims 1 to 6, characterized in that the safety system is a seatbelt assembly with the restraining element being a seatbelt.
